---
title: "[Title]"
author: "Patrick Crawford"
institute: "George Mason University"
date: "[Date/Venue]"
format:
  beamer:
    aspectratio: 169
    fonttheme: structurebold
    navigation: horizontal
    keep-tex: false
    # include-in-header: ../../paper/preambles/beamer-preamble.tex  # your custom preamble
  # revealjs:                   # Uncomment to switch to RevealJS; resolve any Beamer-specific
  #   theme: default            # LaTeX content first (columns environments, \pause, etc.)
  #   css: ../../paper/quarto/custom.scss
  #   slide-number: true
bibliography: ../../references.bib
---

## Research Question

[One-sentence research question.]

## What We Find

- [Main result in plain language]
- [Magnitude and significance]
- [Why it matters]

## Roadmap

1. Motivation & Background
2. Data
3. Empirical Strategy
4. Results
5. Robustness
6. Implications

---

# Motivation

## [Motivating Fact or Puzzle]

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=\textwidth]{../../paper/figures/fig_motivation.pdf}
\end{center}

---

# Data

## Data

[Data description slide]

---

# Empirical Strategy

## Identification

$$
Y_{it} = \alpha + \beta D_{it} + X_{it}'\gamma + \mu_i + \lambda_t + \varepsilon_{it}
$$

- $\beta$: [what it captures]
- Key assumption: [identification assumption]

---

# Results

## Main Result

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=\textwidth]{../../paper/figures/fig_main_result.pdf}
\end{center}

## [Result Interpretation]

- Point estimate: [magnitude]
- [Economic interpretation]
- [Comparison to literature]

---

# Robustness

## [Robustness Check 1]

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=\textwidth]{../../paper/figures/fig_robustness.pdf}
\end{center}

---

# Conclusion

## Takeaway

1. [Main finding]
2. [Policy implication or mechanism]
3. [What's next]

---

# Backup Slides {.appendix}

## Backup: [Topic]

[Content for Q&A]
